dialog.settings.title: Settings
dialog.settings.msg_reboot: Changes to active pack or language will take effect upon next application start.
run.msg.ask_steps: Do you want to input steps (liquid, viscosity, time, etc.) now?
run.label.liquid_hint_new: Can be entered as steps after saving
series.msg.select_series: Select a series from the left list
series.msg.confirm_delete_with_runs:
Series {sid} has {n} run(s) attached.
Deleting it will clear their series_id. Proceed?
steps.msg.viscosity_num: Viscosity must be a number (leave empty if unknown)
steps.msg.row_num: {label} must be a number (leave empty if unknown)
steps.msg.row_order: Data start row is after end row
steps.msg.row_min: Row number must be >= 2 (Row 2 = first data)
steps.msg.num_only: {label} must be a number (leave empty if unknown)
steps.msg.click_wave_first: Please click on the waveform graph first
steps.msg.no_run_id: Cannot edit steps for a run without run_id
steps.msg.select_base: Select a step to use as base
steps.msg.base_prompt_desc: Row number to use as base (processed Excel row)
steps.msg.base_outside_confirm: Row {v} is outside the range ({sr}-{er}) of step {step_no}. Set anyway?
pane.msg.select_run: Select a record to see details
pane.msg.no_series: Series not set (Set series_id in 'Edit Record' to compare)
data.msg.conflict:
The ledger has been modified externally after loading.
(e.g., opened in Excel and saved).
Do you want to overwrite?
data.msg.base_row_invalid: Base row must be >= 2 (Processed Excel row num)
tree.msg.select_raw_path: Select a row (Rows without raw_path are excluded)
wave.msg.click_result: t={t:.2f}min = Row {row} (Copied{filled}. Can paste via '<- Waveform' in step form)
wave.msg.need_mpl: matplotlib is required for waveform preview
wave.msg.no_raw_path: No raw data path (register via edit to see waveform)
wave.msg.span_actual: Step span placed by data rows
wave.msg.click_hint: Click graph -> get row number (Copied + auto-filled. Disabled during zoom/pan)
help.base_correction: Redraws graph using the specified row (base row) as zero point. Cancels initial differences for easy comparison.
help.grade_A: Documented up to interpretation in thesis/slides. Only this grade can be cited as 'established results'.
help.grade_B: Data remains but interpretation is undocumented. Can be reanalyzed, but results are 'own analysis', not established.
help.grade_C: Conditions incomplete/irrecoverable. Cannot be used for causal discussions.
help.understanding: Memo of how well you digested the result. 'Saw result but mechanism unknown' means you can't explain 'why'.
help.adv_filter: Filter list by date range, steps used, etc. Conditions can be saved to presets.
help.data_rows: Which processed Excel rows correspond to this step. Same as SplitStep macro input. Can get by clicking graph.
help.series_tab: Timeline of runs with same series ID, highlighting what changed. Used to confirm single-variable isolation.
help.preset: Save current search conditions with a name. Next time, select from dropdown to reproduce.
main.tooltip.nav_btn: Toggle left navigation pane (Experimenter, Status, etc.)
schema_editor.str6: Built-in packs are read-only. Duplicate one to edit it.
schema_editor.str22: Select a CSV file to test
schema_editor.str34: Enter a name for the new pack:
schema_editor.str38: Enter a name for the new pack:
schema_editor.x_axis_settings: Column Used for the Graph X Axis
schema_editor.channel_settings: Columns Shown on the Graph (Y Axis)
schema_editor.channel_help: Choose the CSV data columns to show on the graph, such as temperature, pressure, or intensity. Use Ctrl or Shift to select multiple columns.
schema_editor.saved_use_pack:
Saved {pack_name}.

Use this pack after restarting Evidex?
schema_editor.restart_to_apply: The pack was selected. Restart Evidex to apply it.
schema_editor.fields_intro: Choose what information is saved for each record. Select an item on the left, then set its displayed name and input method on the right. Finish by clicking Apply to This Item.
schema_editor.field_editor_help: This edits the item selected on the left. If records have already been saved, avoid changing the item ID.
schema_editor.type_text_help: Use for names, notes, and other free-form text.
schema_editor.type_number_help: Use for numeric values such as temperature or concentration.
schema_editor.type_date_help: Use for dates. A calendar button is shown in the record form.
schema_editor.type_choice_help: Use for values selected from a prepared list, such as status or device name.
schema_editor.choices_help: Used only with Choose from a list. Example: New, Analyzed, Complete
schema_editor.display_intro: Choose filters shown beside the record list and optional features used by this pack. Leave unfamiliar features off.
schema_editor.facets_help: Show selected items as filters beside the record list. This works well for repeated values such as researcher, status, or device name. Use Ctrl or Shift to select multiple items.
schema_editor.features_help: Enable only the features you need. All features may remain off when you only need a CSV list and waveform display.
schema_editor.feature_steps_help: Record multiple steps such as adding a sample, waiting, or washing.
schema_editor.feature_series: Group related records into a series
schema_editor.feature_series_help: Compare multiple measurement records as one research series.
schema_editor.feature_grading: Grade records as A, B, or C
schema_editor.feature_grading_help: Classify record reliability or usability. Enabling this also allows grade colors to be configured.
schema_editor.feature_baseline_help: Display waveform changes relative to a selected reference position.
schema_editor.invalid_name: Use only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores in pack names.
schema_editor.invalid_fields: RUN_FIELDS must contain unique, non-empty field IDs.
schema_editor.invalid_field_id: Use only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores in field IDs.
schema_editor.run_id_required: The run_id field cannot be deleted.
schema_editor.invalid_skip: Rows to skip must be zero or a positive integer.
schema_editor.invalid_delimiter: The delimiter must be exactly one character.
schema_editor.adapter_columns_required: Enter an X-axis column and at least one channel column.
schema_editor.invalid_color: Grade {grade} must use a six-digit hex color such as #1E7A3C.
schema_editor.csv_guidance_template: This is not made for a specific instrument. It is a template for creating a new pack. To use your own CSV, duplicate this pack and configure the reading method below.
schema_editor.csv_guidance_custom_reader: This pack includes its own CSV reader. Ask the pack provider which CSV formats it supports.
schema_editor.csv_guidance_column_settin: Configure how this pack reads CSV files below. If columns are already shown, those settings have been saved.
schema_editor.current_settings_empty: No CSV settings have been saved yet.
schema_editor.current_settings_incomplet: CSV settings are not complete yet.
schema_editor.current_settings_next_step: Next: 1. Choose a sample CSV  2. Pick the X axis and graph columns  3. Apply CSV Settings  4. Test Import  5. Save
schema_editor.current_reader_custom_loca: Saved settings: X axis, graph columns, and units are defined inside adapter.py.
schema_editor.current_reader_custom_mapp: Column mapping below: used for adapter_config.json packs. Empty fields here do not mean this custom-reader pack is unconfigured.
schema_editor.new_pack_tip: Create an empty user pack for a new instrument or CSV format.
schema_editor.duplicate_tip: Copy the selected pack into your user area so it can be edited.
schema_editor.delete_tip: Delete the selected user pack. Built-in packs cannot be deleted.
schema_editor.save_tip: Save this pack and optionally select it for the next Evidex restart.
schema_editor.choose_csv_tip: Pick a representative CSV so Evidex can detect columns and delimiter.
schema_editor.reload_columns_tip: Read the selected sample CSV again after changing rows to skip or delimiter.
schema_editor.apply_adapter_tip: Apply the selected X axis, graph columns, units, and CSV options to this pack.
schema_editor.test_adapter_tip: Preview the graph and first rows before saving the pack.
qt.run.confirm_delete:
Delete experiment record "{run_id}"?

This removes the record from runs.csv.
A copy of the CSV is saved in the backup folder first.
qt.schema_packs.copy_name_prompt: Name for a copy of "{source}" (letters, numbers, _ and - only):
qt.waveform.preview_error: Could not create the CSV table preview: {error}
qt.common.select_an_experiment_record_fr: Select an experiment record from the table on the left.
qt.common.registered_files_appear_here_a: Registered files appear here after you select an experiment record.
qt.common.the_raw_path_csv_and_a_quick_g: The raw_path CSV and a quick graph appear here after you select an experiment record.
qt.common.this_series_is_not_registered_: This series is not registered in series.csv. You can register it in Series Manager.
qt.common.no_files_are_registered_for_th: No files are registered for this record.
qt.common.this_is_the_qt_preview_the_tki: This is the Qt preview. The Tkinter version remains available.
qt.common.search_by_id_date_title_summar: Search by ID, date, title, summary, file path, and more
qt.common.select_an_experiment_record_to: Select an experiment record to edit.
Select an experiment record to delete.
qt.common.select_an_experiment_record_wh: Select an experiment record whose steps you want to edit.
qt.common.steps_cannot_be_edited_for_a_r: Steps cannot be edited for a record without a run_id.
qt.common.series_manager_changes_have_be: Series Manager changes have been applied.
qt.common.group_experiment_records_by_se: Group experiment records by series_id to review research objectives and claims.
qt.common.there_are_no_series_yet_create: There are no series yet. Create one or set series_id on an experiment record.
qt.common.select_a_series_from_the_list_: Select a series from the list on the left.
qt.common.series_information_could_not_b: Series information could not be displayed.
qt.common.not_registered_in_series_csv_u: Not registered in series.csv. Use Edit Series Info to create it.
qt.common.title: Title
qt.common.edit_the_steps_linked_to_this_: Edit the steps linked to this experiment record in the table. Step numbers are assigned from top to bottom when saved.
qt.common.this_pack_does_not_define_any_: This pack does not define any step fields. Add step fields in the pack settings to use steps.
qt.common.select_a_csv_file_to_display_a: Select a CSV file to display a matplotlib graph here.
qt.common.no_csv_is_registered_in_raw_pa: No CSV is registered in raw_path. Edit the experiment record and add a CSV to display its table and graph here.
qt.common.matplotlib_is_required_for_hig: matplotlib is required for high-quality graph display.

matplotlib is required for high-quality graph display.
Install it with: python -m pip install matplotlib
qt.common.there_is_no_numeric_data_to_gr: There is no numeric data to graph.
qt.common.there_is_not_enough_numeric_da: There is not enough numeric data to graph.
qt.common.click_the_graph_to_copy_the_co: Click the graph to copy the corresponding CSV row number to the clipboard.
qt.common.this_csv_cannot_be_read_with_t: This CSV cannot be read with the current pack's graph settings. You can inspect it in the table preview.
